# Architecture Analyzer Output

**Platform**: OpenShift AI  
**Generated**: 2026-05-17T04:27:56Z  
**Components**: 34  

## How to Find Information

| Question Type | Where to Look |
|---------------|---------------|
| "What CRDs does X manage?" | `components/<name>.md` -> CRDs section |
| "What ports does X use?" | `components/<name>.md` -> Services section |
| "How does X interact with Y?" | `cross-component/interactions.md` or `components/<name>.md` -> Interactions section |
| "Where is the X component doc?" | `components/<name>.md` |
| "What dependencies does X have?" | `components/<name>.md` -> Dependencies section |
| "What RBAC does X need?" | `components/<name>.md` -> RBAC section |
| Platform-level overview | `PLATFORM.md` |
| Full structured data | `platform-architecture.json` |
| Detailed component data (JSON) | `<name>/component-architecture.json` |
| Code property graph (functions, calls, file paths) | `<name>/code-graph.json` |
| Cross-component interactions (deep dive) | Grep `<name>/code-graph.json` for other component names in file paths |

## Per-Component Raw Data

Each component has a subdirectory with structured JSON extracted by static analysis:

```
<component-name>/
  component-architecture.json   # Full extracted data: CRDs, services, RBAC,
                                # deployments, operator config, reconcile sequences,
                                # controller watches, and more
  code-graph.json               # Code property graph: functions, classes, calls,
                                # file paths. Use file paths to discover cross-
                                # component integrations (e.g., a path like
                                # providers/remote/inference/vllm/ indicates
                                # integration with the vllm component)
```

Use these for deep-dive queries when the markdown docs don't have enough detail.
